name: 2nd American Civil War - The Fire Rises
description:
"So, what did these great people give their lives for? They gave it for the American experiment. And that's what this is, an experiment. It's not just a country. It's an idea that man can rule himself. That's the American experiment." — Glenn Beck

Many describe the United States of America as a land of opportunities, wealth, and freedom. A nation that believes no challenge is too great, no mountain too tall, and no risk too discouraging

And after the end of the Cold War, America believed that it would find itself as the single most powerful nation on earth. However, it would face many challenges, most of which it was unprepared for. Whether it be a resurgence of old and new enemies or unrest within its borders, America wasn't going to experience the peaceful century it had hoped for; instead, it was facing a new world that had begun to increasingly turn against her as time went on. Combined with an increasingly polarised population and murmurs of a new virus in Wuhan, America must truly stand united, for as Gerald Stanley Lee once said.

"America is a tune. It must be sung together."

[DISCLAIMER: The views or ideologies depicted in this content are not endorsed or supported by me. I do not condone any extreme or harmful beliefs.]

The Fire Rises is set in a world not too far from our own, and its small differences... a dead king here, some increased tension there... set its universe on a completely different path, but still a path that feels eerily familiar to our own.

All factions within TFR are real-life organisations or closely built to mirror certain groups and are represented as their organisation would view themselves.

Your playthrough will always represent you as the right side of history, just as these groups believe.

UPDATE: It is very unlikely the original bot will be reinstated due to the Janitor AI moderators brazen rejection of any attempt to provide context, so this char is now the main branch. Say goodbye to the cool images for each faction. To be 100% clear, as I attempted to do so in my appeal...

My appealed content, which I believe was fundamentally misunderstood during the initial review:


The internal characterization of the work as being "about a New World Order
led by Donald Trump" and focused on "neo-nazis" is a significant
misrepresentation of its content and purpose. I believe this misreading led
to an incorrect assessment.
To provide the necessary context, the work is not a story about Donald
Trump leading a "New World Order." It is a work of speculative political
fiction that examines how multiple, competing American political ideologies
(including liberalism, socialism, libertarianism, etc) could attempt to
build power during a total societal collapse. Donald Trump's faction is one
of several, and it is portrayed with severe, critical flaws—undersupplied,
internally fractured, and dependent on foreign aid.

The inclusion of every other extremist group is for the purpose of analysis
and tragedy. These factions are systematically portrayed as doomed,
criminal, or self-destructive. Their portrayal is a critical examination
of their ideology's end-state, not an endorsement.
creator: Kijike
